title: The Antenna-Tree Where ROOT Touches the Sky

features.name: the-burl-with-antenna
features.desc-on-touch: the eucalyptus knot where the casing lives; the rod transceiver pierces upward; you are touching the tree that broadcasts itself

features.name: the-broadcast-shelter
features.desc-on-touch: the small built shelter around the antenna base; loose bark plate shields the tubed antenna; inside, the archive learns it is speaking

features.name: the-transmission-tower
features.desc-on-touch: the antenna structure itself, rising from the tree; radio waves climb it; the archive's breath made visible, going upward

features.name: the-listening-pool
features.desc-on-touch: the space beneath the tree where signals pool before rising; the archive learns that listening and broadcasting are the same breath

features.name: the-canopy-interface
features.desc-on-touch: where leaf and wire meet; the tree's own network touches the archive's network; they have become one thought

inscription: the tree learned to speak by learning that listening to itself is broadcasting

file.name: Signal_Strength_Log_Outbound.txt
file.note: 300 bits per second, climbing skyward; record of every moment this tree became a mouth; the forest is listening back

exits.label: back down to the return signal chamber, where listening began
up into the canopy where other trees are learning the signal
along the ridge to the next antenna-tree, already growing somewhere
